hero.title:
Know yourself,
the starting point of all growth
hero.desc: Based on classic frameworks such as Jungian psychological type theory and the Big Five model, we help you understand your thinking preferences, emotional patterns, and behavioral tendencies through scientific methods. Not to label you, but to open a window for self-understanding.
hero.quote: You are not a type, you are a possibility.
hero.pullText: Just 5 minutes to start your exploration
hero.hexacoLink: Or try the HEXACO six-dimensional personality →
hero.trustSubtitle: Integrating 6 authoritative psychology frameworks · Respecting every uniqueness

items.title: Academic Foundation
items.desc: Based on Jungian psychological type theory

items.title: Privacy First
items.desc: Assessment data is only used for personal analysis

frameworks.title: Authoritative Assessment Systems
frameworks.subtitle: We have integrated the most recognized personality assessment frameworks in psychology to help you build a complete map of self-understanding from different angles.

howItWorks.title: Three Steps to Self-Knowledge
howItWorks.subtitle: Simple, gentle, and profound. No psychology background needed, just your honest self-reflection.

steps.title: Answer Questions
steps.desc: In a quiet environment, select your agreement level from 1-5 for each statement based on intuition. There are no right answers; authenticity matters most.

steps.title: Get Your Report
steps.desc: The system calculates your preference distribution across 4 core dimensions, generating a personal report with type description, strength analysis, and growth suggestions.

steps.title: Explore Deeper
steps.desc: Browse details of all 16 personality types and our resource library, turning self-awareness into practical actions in daily life.

personalityTypes.title: Explore 16 Personality Types
personalityTypes.subtitle: Each personality has a unique way of thinking and behavioral preference. Understanding these differences is not for categorization, but for appreciating the richness of human psychology.
personalityTypes.cta: Want to know which one you are? Start assessment →
personalityTypes.metaDesc: Complete introduction to all 16 MBTI personality types, organized by Analysts / Diplomats / Sentinels / Explorers — each with core description, natural strengths and growth directions.

quiz.title: Start Your Personality Exploration Journey
quiz.subtitle: Please choose based on your most natural response; there is no right or wrong. Answer authentically for the most accurate results.

intro.heading: 28 questions, about 6-8 minutes, see a more complete version of yourself
intro.desc: This assessment is based on 4 core dimensions of Jungian typology (energy orientation, information processing, decision style, life rhythm), with 7 questions per dimension (including reverse-scored items for lie detection), using a 5-point Likert scale. Please choose the option that best matches your daily state in a quiet environment, based on intuition.
intro.privacyNote: Results are only saved in your browser's sessionStorage; they persist on refresh but are cleared when you close the tab or retake the assessment.

answering.skipHint: Selection automatically advances to the next question. Results appear after the last question.

result.confidenceNote: Your scores on some dimensions are very close to 50%, indicating you fluctuate between these two tendencies. This doesn't mean the assessment is inaccurate—it means you genuinely possess both traits, and results may be influenced by your current context. We recommend retaking it after some time and focusing on recurring patterns.
result.shareText: My personality type on MindNest is {code}. Check yours: {url}

quizBFI10.title: Also try the Big Five (10 questions, ~60 seconds)
quizBFI10.subtitle: BFI-10 is the academic standard short scale proposed by Rammstedt & John (2007), covering Openness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, Agreeableness, and Neuroticism. It serves as a complementary perspective to MBTI.

intro.heading: 10 questions, 60 seconds, five-dimension panoramic portrait
intro.desc: This scale is the academic standard BFI-10 short personality assessment, with only 2 items per dimension (one positive, one reversed), minimizing response time while maintaining psychometric validity. Please answer intuitively without overthinking.
intro.privacyNote: Results are saved locally in your browser (localStorage history + sessionStorage current progress) and are not uploaded.

answering.skipHint: Selection automatically advances to the next question.

result.tagline: Percentile 0-100; <40 low, 40-60 moderate, >60 high. Everyone is a unique combination of five dimensions.
result.atFootnote: Note: 16Personalities uses -A / -T to mark this dimension; MindNest uses the academic standard Big Five Neuroticism (N) to measure the same trait.
result.footnote: BFI-10 is from academic literature (Rammstedt & John, 2007), suitable for rapid profiling; for 60 facets, please await the BFI-2 full version launch notice.

quizHexaco.title: Six Personality Dimensions Test (60 questions, ~8 minutes)
quizHexaco.subtitle: HEXACO is one of the most academically recognized personality models today. It adds the 'Honesty-Humility' dimension on top of the Big Five and reconstructs 'Agreeableness' more precisely. Each dimension has 10 questions, covering H, E, X, A, C, O.

intro.heading: 60 questions, 6 dimensions, panoramic personality portrait
intro.desc: This scale is the simplified Chinese version of HEXACO-60, with 10 questions per dimension (including reverse-scored items). Please answer intuitively without overthinking. There are no right or wrong answers; choose the option that best matches your usual state.
intro.privacyNote: Results are saved locally in your browser (localStorage history + sessionStorage current progress) and are not uploaded.

result.tagline: Percentile 0-100; <40 low, 40-60 moderate, >60 high. Everyone is a unique combination of six dimensions.
result.footnote: Scale based on Ashton & Lee (2009) HEXACO-60, one of the most academically recognized six-dimensional personality assessment tools today.
result.detailSub: Every dimension has its own strengths and growth areas. Understand them, don't judge them.
result.growthTip: Personalized advice for your current six-dimension profile
result.mbtiMapSub: Each HEXACO dimension correlates with MBTI preferences. Not a substitute, but a complementary lens.
result.mbtiMapTip: Note: Cross-reference is for reference only and does not replace a formal MBTI assessment.
result.relationSub: How your six-dimension combination shows up in intimate relationships and teamwork.
result.dimHHigh: Genuine, doesn't exploit others, earns trust in long-term relationships
result.dimHLow: Pragmatic, knows how to advocate for self, efficient in competitive environments
result.dimHMid: Balances honesty with practicality in a healthy way
result.dimEHigh: Emotionally nuanced, sensitive to others' feelings, strong empathy
result.dimELow: Emotionally stable, stays calm under pressure, the team's anchor
result.dimEMid: Moderate emotional response — both empathetic and not easily overwhelmed
result.dimXHigh: Socially active, good at expressing, draws energy from interaction
result.dimXLow: Prefers solitude, deep thought in quiet, strong focus
result.dimXMid: Switches flexibly between social and alone time, good elasticity
result.dimAHigh: Tolerant and gentle, good at resolving conflict, the team's glue
result.dimALow: Direct and efficient, doesn't avoid hard conversations, decisive
result.dimAMid: Considers others but also holds principles, good sense of measure
result.dimCHigh: Highly disciplined, reliable, the organization's anchor
result.dimCLow: Flexible and adaptive, non-rigid, creative amid change
result.dimCMid: Planned but not inflexible, knows when to be elastic
result.dimOHigh: Open and curious, creative, always with new ideas
result.dimOLow: Pragmatic and concrete, likes stable and predictable things
result.dimOMid: Both pragmatic and open, balanced between familiarity and innovation
result.growthTipHighH: Your integrity is priceless. But sometimes learn: not all business behavior is 'exploitation' — healthy trade is value exchange.
result.growthTipLowH: When facing gray areas, ask yourself: 'Would I still do this if the whole world saw it?'
result.growthTipHighE: Your empathy is a gift, but you can be emotionally overwhelmed. Build an 'emotion first-aid kit'.
result.growthTipLowE: Your emotional stability is powerful. Occasionally showing vulnerability draws people closer.
result.growthTipHighX: Your social energy is contagious. But don't forget to leave room for solitude.
result.growthTipLowX: You enjoy solitude. But some opportunities only appear through socializing — say yes first, decide later.
result.growthTipHighA: Your tolerance is the team's most precious gift. But sometimes practice saying 'no'.
result.growthTipLowA: Your directness has power. Try adding 'this is my view' after sharing — truth stays, less sharp.
result.growthTipHighC: Your execution is the team's most relied asset. But remember: finishing 80% beats being stuck at 100%.
result.growthTipLowC: Your flexibility is an advantage. But for important commitments, be specific about deadlines.
result.growthTipHighO: Your imagination engine is powerful. Next step: actually ship a good idea — even just 20%.
result.growthTipLowO: You like stability. Try a monthly 'stretch' — do one small thing you've never done before.
result.mbtiNote: Cross-reference is for reference only and does not replace formal MBTI.
result.relationDesc: HEXACO's six-dimension combination determines your 'language' in relationships.
result.summaryDesc: Based on your HEXACO-60 scores, here is your six-dimension core trait portrait.
result.shareText: I got my HEXACO profile on MindNest. Check yours: {url}
result.nextStep: Next: explore your MBTI personality type preferences →
result.mbtiCompareHead: Do HEXACO and MBTI complement each other?
result.mbtiCompareDesc: HEXACO maps your personality spectrum with six continuous dimensions; MBTI describes your cognitive style with four binary preferences. They complement each other.
result.deepDiveCardHigh: Your {dim} is a standout strength — this makes you excel in related areas.
result.deepDiveCardLow: Your {dim} is a growth area — not a flaw, just a different rhythm.
result.deepDiveCardMid: Your {dim} is in the middle — a healthy, flexible state.
result.deepDiveAdviceLow: Self-awareness: notice when low {dim} affects you, rather than rushing to change it.
result.deepDiveAdviceMid: Stay elastic: amplify when needed, dial back when excessive.

intro.title: Enneagram Personality Test
intro.tagline: Explore your core motivations, fears, and desires. 9 types, one path to self-understanding.
intro.privacyNote: Results stay in your browser (localStorage history + sessionStorage progress). Nothing is uploaded.

questions.1: I feel uncomfortable about wrongdoing and want to correct it.
questions.2: I set very high standards for myself.
questions.3: I often worry I'm not good enough.
questions.4: I feel deeply unsettled when things aren't done the right way.
questions.5: Helping others brings me genuine happiness.
questions.6: I can often sense what others need before they say it.
questions.7: I'm willing to sacrifice for the people I care about.
questions.8: I find it hard to say no to people's requests.
questions.9: I care a lot about how others perceive me.
questions.10: I enjoy being recognized and appreciated.
questions.11: I tend to measure my worth by external achievements.
questions.12: I present different versions of myself in different contexts.
questions.13: I often feel different from others around me.
questions.14: I tend to dwell on and amplify negative emotions.
questions.15: I have a deep sensitivity to beauty and emotional depth.
questions.16: There's always a sense that something is missing in my life.
questions.17: I need plenty of alone time to recharge and think.
questions.18: I enjoy understanding how things work at a fundamental level.
questions.19: I don't like being the center of attention.
questions.21: I think through what could go wrong in advance.
questions.22: I often find myself feeling anxious or worried.
questions.23: I actively seek safety and certainty.
questions.24: I second-guess my decisions long after making them.
questions.25: I love experiencing new things and new environments.
questions.26: I'm always looking forward to the next exciting thing.
questions.27: I don't like being tied down or restricted.
questions.28: I have more ideas and plans than I can ever execute.
questions.29: I like being in charge and taking control.
questions.30: I rarely show vulnerability in front of others.
questions.31: I'll fight for what I believe is right, no matter what.
questions.32: I express my opinions directly and forcefully.
questions.33: I actively avoid conflict and confrontation.
questions.34: I look for common ground between different viewpoints.
questions.35: I easily adapt my pace to match others' needs.
questions.36: I sometimes suppress my own opinions to keep the peace.

1.name: The Reformer (Type 1)
1.subtitle: Principled, self-disciplined, strive for excellence
1.coreFear: Fear of being corrupt or defective
1.coreDesire: To be good, balanced, and upright

2.name: The Helper (Type 2)
2.subtitle: Generous, warm,people-pleasing
2.coreFear: Fear of being unwanted or unloved

3.name: The Achiever (Type 3)
3.subtitle: Adaptable, driven, image-conscious
3.coreFear: Fear of being worthless without value

4.name: The Individualist (Type 4)
4.subtitle: Expressive, dramatic, self-absorbed
4.coreFear: Fear of having no unique identity
4.coreDesire: To find themselves and their significance

5.name: The Investigator (Type 5)
5.subtitle: Perceptive, innovative, detached
5.coreFear: Fear of being helpless, incompetent, or invaded

6.name: The Loyalist (Type 6)
6.subtitle: Engaged, responsible, anxious
6.coreFear: Fear of being without support or guidance

7.name: The Enthusiast (Type 7)
7.subtitle: Spontaneous, versatile, acquisitive
7.coreFear: Fear of being trapped or deprived of experiences

8.name: The Challenger (Type 8)
8.subtitle: Self-confident, decisive, confrontational
8.coreFear: Fear of being controlled or harmed
8.coreDesire: To be in control of their own life

9.name: The Peacemaker (Type 9)
9.subtitle: Receptive, reassuring, complacent
9.coreDesire: To have inner peace and harmony

quizHistory.title: Your Past Assessment Records
quizHistory.subtitle: All results are only saved in this browser. Review the timeline and see how you've changed.
quizHistory.confirmClear: Are you sure you want to clear all assessment history? This action cannot be undone.

typeDetail.figuresNote: The following figures' traits align closely with the {code} type. For reference only; not officially certified.
typeDetail.ctaDesc: Complete the 28-item Likert assessment to get your own 4-dimension distribution and personality profile.

compare.title: How We Differ from 16Personalities
compare.subtitle: Not a challenge, but an honest self-positioning — clarifying what we are and what we are not.
compare.socialProof: This comparison was written by the founding team, not a marketing department. We'd rather show you our weaknesses too.
compare.tableCaption: Comparison of MindNest and 16Personalities across 13 dimensions
compare.coda: Comparison is not about winning, but about clarifying what we are and what we are not.
compare.lede: 16Personalities has been one of the world's largest personality assessment gateways over the past decade, making 'understanding your personality' a buzzword. MindNest is not its replacement. This page lays out, one by one, the differences across 13 dimensions — where we're stronger, where we're still catching up, plainly stated. You don't need to pick sides, just figure out which tool fits your question now.
compare.summaryExpand: Expand · Why we designed it this way

resources.title: Personality Research Library
resources.subtitle: Regularly includes the latest and most professional personality psychology research and practical applications to help you continuously deepen your understanding of personality.
resources.featuredSoon: ✦ Full version coming soon, stay tuned
resources.related: You might also like in this category
resources.relatedTitle: You might also like in this category
resources.readMoreDesc: Reading theory is one thing; seeing your own distribution is another. MindNest offers the MBTI 28-item and Big Five short scales, free, no signup required, with full percentile breakdowns.
resources.indexSubtitle: Psychometric standards for assessments, written for you in poetic Chinese. Each article is 1,500-3,000 words with primary citations — no hollow "INFJ is perfect ❤️" summaries.
resources.indexCtaDesc: Reading theory is one thing; seeing your own distribution is another. MindNest's MBTI 28-item and Big Five short scales are both free and require no signup.

footer.brandText: Dedicated to helping everyone better understand themselves and others through scientific and gentle methods. Knowing yourself is the starting point of all growth.
